element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns dodge, jump timing, basic sword attack patterns against soldier-type enemies
Limited control in single-player; full cooperative control in two-player mode. Falcon assists with hitting distant targets and breaking obstacles
Player receives Black Sword after defeating Spain boss. Must learn each boss's attack pattern. Realizes swords are tied to boss vulnerabilities
Red Sword effective against Egypt boss, White Sword against Germany boss, etc. Player must strategize level order or use hints
Seven cryptic clues hidden in levels reveal optimal stage order and final jewel arrangement at Altar of Peace
More aggressive enemy types with varied attack patterns. Introduce damage spike difficulty
Player can tackle any of remaining 7 castles in chosen order, but boss vulnerability to specific swords creates optimal path
Mandatory final stage with ultimate boss encounter. Requires all previously acquired swords and mastery

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After House of Ruth, at Altar of Peace
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player must arrange 8 jewels in correct order based on collected clues or loses game. No combat; pure logic finale

typical_tension_triggers: Incorrect sword vs. boss (minimal damage feedback)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Castlevania-like rhythm: sustained exploration tension punctuated by intense boss encounters; multiple level-choice paths allow player-controlled tension pacing; climax builds through mandatory House of Ruth and logic puzzle finale

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Clue scrolls hidden off main path; collectibles reward thorough exploration of each castle
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden clue scrolls scattered throughout 7 castles; players can discover hints about optimal stage order and final jewel puzzle solution through exploration or trial-and-error

difficulty_curve.early_game: Spain (Level 1) introduces basic platforming, falcon mechanics, and boss structure; relatively gentle learning curve with standard soldiers as primary threats
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Levels 2-7 (Egypt through Germany) escalate enemy variety and introduce nuclear mutants; player freedom to choose level order allows self-directed difficulty scaling; sword-to-boss matching puzzle adds strategic layer
difficulty_curve.late_game: House of Ruth combines combat mastery check with all previous swords required; final jewel arrangement puzzle shifts from action to pure logic, requiring collected clues or memory of hints
notable_spikes: First boss encounter (Spain) - introduces dodge-heavy patterns
Nuclear mutant introduction (mid-levels) - enemy aggression spike
House of Ruth mandatory sequence - density and complexity increase
Jewel arrangement puzzle - instant loss condition if incorrect order chosen

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of 8 levels is structured as infiltrating a Duke's castle with soldier enemies, environmental hazards, and a boss guardian at the end. Progression is fundamentally linear within each stage but players choose order across Levels 1-7
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Spain (Level 1) - introduces template; Egypt, Arabia, Italy, India, Africa, Germany follow same structure

notable_level_patterns.description: Boss defeat grants new sword. New sword is optimized for a specific boss elsewhere (not the defeated one). Creates puzzle: player discovers through hints or experimentation which sword matches which future boss, creating optimal level sequencing
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Red Sword (Egypt boss) must be used against Arabia boss; order matters for efficiency

notable_level_patterns.description: Seven cryptic clues hidden in 7 castles reveal (1) optimal level order and (2) final puzzle solution. Clues are optional but strongly encouraged, signaling non-linear exploration value
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Distributed across Spain, Egypt, Arabia, Italy, India, Africa, Germany

notable_level_patterns.description: House of Ruth (action finale) followed by Altar of Peace (logic finale). Shifts from combat mastery to puzzle-solving, preventing rote button-mashing approach to ending
notable_level_patterns.example_level: House of Ruth → Altar of Peace sequence
